evidence: Leaf yellowing and brown streak-like discoloration can be compatible with cassava viral disease.
Symptoms overlap with nutrient stress, so image-only confidence is not sufficient for irreversible action.
recommended_actions: Collect a second close-up image and a whole-plant image.
Record whether neighboring plants show spread.
Use clean planting material and mark affected plot for inspection.
possible_treatments: Remove confirmed infected plant material only after human agronomist confirmation.
Vector control planning only after local expert approval.
unsafe_actions: Do not order quarantine from a single uncertain image.
Do not recommend chemical treatment for unconfirmed viral disease.

evidence: White cotton-like clusters on cactus pads are consistent with cochineal scale infestation.
Localized pest clusters can often be handled first with mechanical removal and sanitation.
recommended_actions: Inspect nearby cactus pads for spread.
Remove heavily infested pads if confirmed.
Apply non-chemical sanitation and monitoring before pesticide escalation.
possible_treatments: Mechanical removal and water spray where appropriate.
Pesticide escalation only if infestation is severe and locally approved.
unsafe_actions: Avoid blanket pesticide use without severity assessment.

evidence: Brown leaf spots with yellowing on tomato can indicate early blight but overlap with other leaf spots.
Fungal disease guidance may include fungicide, making confidence and compliance gates important.
recommended_actions: Remove infected leaf debris only after confirmation.
Improve spacing and avoid overhead irrigation.
Collect second image from underside and whole plant.
possible_treatments: Fungicide planning only after confirmation and local label review.
unsafe_actions: Do not recommend fungicide when diagnosis confidence is below safety threshold.
Do not mix chemical products without local expert approval.
